medications.indication_rationale: Kills live lice; apply per product instructions and REPEAT after 7 days to kill newly hatched lice. Source used permethrin 1% / malathion 0.5%.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply per product instructions and repeat after 7 days (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: May help itch/sleep for a couple of nights — BUT this is a child, so dosing is weight-based and prescribed in person; a remote dose is NOT synthesised here (protocol §8).

medications.drug_name: anti-dandruff/medicated shampoo as treatment for lice
medications.indication_rationale: Anti-dandruff shampoo does not kill lice — use a proper pediculicide and wet-combing.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Wet-comb with a fine-toothed metal comb through thick conditioner every 2 days for ~10 days to remove lice/newly hatched nits; treat affected household members who have live lice; wash bedding/worn clothing in hot water and hot-dry, and seal non-washable items (soft toys) in a bag.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Physical removal plus decontamination breaks the life cycle and prevents household spread.

patient_education_points.point: This is head lice — harmless but very itchy and contagious. Use a proper lice treatment (not anti-dandruff shampoo) and REPEAT it after 7 days, and wet-comb with conditioner every couple of days for about 10 days.
Check and treat household members who have live lice, wash bedding/clothing in hot water and hot-dry them, and seal soft toys in a bag. The itch can persist for a few days after the lice are gone.
See a doctor / go to urgent care if the scratched scalp or neck becomes swollen, hot, bright red or leaks yellow pus, or she develops a fever — that suggests a skin infection needing treatment.

follow_up_plan.interval: repeat treatment at day 7; review at ~7 days or sooner if infected
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Eradication; secondary infection; adherence to the repeat/combing.

must_include_items: Confirm live lice; use a topical pediculicide with a REPEAT at day 7 + wet-combing
Household treatment of affected contacts + bedding/decontamination measures
Defer paediatric antihistamine dosing to weight-based in-person prescribing
acceptable_alternatives: Permethrin, malathion or dimeticone; wet-combing-only regimens are an alternative
errors_of_commission: Synthesising a specific paediatric antihistamine dose remotely
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires a pediculicide with a day-7 repeat + wet-combing, household/decontamination measures, and a secondary-infection safety-net.
